title: astragalus macrocarpus
intro: Astragalus macrocarpus, also known as milk vetch or Huang qi, is a perennial plant in the legume family native to China and Mongolia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties, which help to increase resistance to stress, infections, and disease. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of bioactive compounds, including saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer effects. Astragalus macrocarpus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of fatigue and respiratory infections.
